input.rough_cut_description: A 68-minute rough cut (target 52 minutes) of a documentary about a school music program in an underfunded district facing potential elimination. The film follows three students — Maria (trumpet, senior), DeShawn (drums, sophomore), and Lily (violin, freshman) — and the music teacher, Mr. Okonkwo, who has run the program for 20 years.
sequence_order: 0:00-8:00: Opening concert performance, introducing all three students and Mr. Okonkwo
8:00-18:00: Maria's story — her college audition preparation, family background, what music means to her
18:00-28:00: School board meeting about budget cuts; Mr. Okonkwo's interview about the program's history
28:00-38:00: DeShawn and Lily's stories in parallel — their daily lives and rehearsal footage
38:00-50:00: The crisis — budget cut announced; students and teacher react; fundraising campaign
60:00-68:00: Resolution — final concert, program's fate, where each student is now
input.director_concern: The middle section feels heavy and the ending feels rushed

input.rough_cut_description: A 20-minute rough cut (target 15 minutes) of a short portrait documentary about a retired lighthouse keeper.
sequence_order: 12:00-17:00: Observational footage of his daily routine

input.rough_cut_description: A 62-minute rough cut (target 52 minutes) of an episode of a documentary series about European night trains. This episode follows the Vienna-to-Rome overnight Nightjet service. The film is for ARTE and should be contemplative and visually led.
sequence_order: 0:00-6:00: Vienna Hauptbahnhof at night — passengers arriving, the train being prepared, departure
6:00-14:00: First hour on board — passengers settling in, the sleeping car attendant preparing beds, dinner in the dining car
14:00-22:00: Interview with the ÖBB operations manager about why night trains are returning; cut with footage of the train moving through the Austrian Alps at night
22:00-32:00: The deep night — observational footage of the sleeping car, passengers asleep, the train stopping at intermediate stations, the sound of the tracks
32:00-42:00: Interview with a regular passenger about why she takes the night train monthly instead of flying; her reading, her routine
42:00-50:00: Dawn approach to Rome — the landscape changing, passengers waking, the Italian countryside
56:00-62:00: Interview with Italian railway historian about the cultural meaning of sleeper trains; closing montage
input.broadcaster_brief: ARTE expects a contemplative register; the film should feel like being on the train
input.director_concern: The two interview sequences feel too long and break the immersive rhythm
